rulings.Case Title: EST. OF DER HONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Approving Assignment Of Interests In Estate, And Approval Of Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/27/2024. The petition appears premature for the court's review at this time. Each participant in the settlement agreement executed the document in an individual capacity, not in the capacity of any court-appointed fiduciary. The petition does not provide under which authority the court can grant the assignment executed by the individual assignors whose interests have not been adjudicated. A (successor) fiduciary needs to be appointed in the decedent's estate (#216027, #21608, #299969, and #303882) and the 1988 Jair Trust (#304547) so that the fiduciary can report the assets subject to the administration of each case and to properly close the administration. The court is not in a position to approve a post-distribution agreement among potential ultimate successors-in-interest by bypassing all statutory requirements for the administration of the decedent's estate. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JESUS PULE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/13/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Still no proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Original bank statements still have not been filed. (3) It is unclear how the bondable liquid assets and one year's worth of income at the end of the first account period were determined to be $237,575.21 and $23,008.35, respectively. The second amended first account showed $238,619.62 in cash on hand and receipts totaling $18,386.08. (4) The second account period showed a total of $58,324.94 in receipts. Deducting the $20 returned and $1,200 stimulus check would result in $57,104.94 for those two years, or $28,552.47 for one year's income...? (5) The amount stated in paragraph 9 to be the balance at the end of this third account period ($12,047.34) does not appear to be correct. (6) Attorney costs paid on 5/12/22 and 12/12/22 do not appear to have been ordered or itemized. (7) There are reimbursements to Julie Cantu without identifying who the original payees were. (8) The Court has no jurisdiction over the trust. Probate Code Section 2646 does not authorize the Court to order a trustee to pay fees; it merely says that the Court shall only determine fees payable from the estate of the conservatee and not limit fees payable from other sources. Probate Code Section 17200 concerns the internal affairs of a trust. (9) Under what authority can the Court authorize the co-conservators to transfer assets to the trustee of the conservatee's trust? Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/4/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Letters were first issued to the co-conservators on 10/23/17, not 10/5/17. Transfer of cash between accounts is not a change in the FORM of assets that is reported on an informational schedule. [Probate Code Section 1063(b).)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM DORSEY STUART JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement And Mutual General Release
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The settlement petition requests the Court's order retaining jurisdiction to enforce the settlement under CCP section 664.6. Shortly after the parties settled in September 2023, Giliane (Nikki) Balgos, a party to the settlement, dismissed with prejudice her case-opening petition for probate of the decedent's 2022 will contested by petitioner Beverly Gipson. Although the superseded settlement petition filed by Beverly Gipson before Nikki Balgos' dismissal prayed for the Court's retention of jurisdiction under section 664.6, Nikki Balgos and Ricardo Maglaya did not sign a stipulation to join in the request for the retention of jurisdiction until January 2024. Paragraph 4 of the September 2023 superseded settlement agreement and paragraph 4 of the January 2024 amended settlement agreement both provide in relevant part that: "Nikki and Ricardo shall stipulate to and join in the relief requested in the Petition. Said Petition will request an order --- retaining jurisdiction to enforce the provisions of this Settlement Agreement pursuant to C.C.P. section 664.6." Paragraph 14 of the September 2023 superseded settlement agreement and paragraph 15 of the January 2024 amended settlement agreement provide in relevant part that: "The Parties shall jointly request through stipulation that the Court will retain jurisdiction to enforce this Agreement under the provisions of California Code of Civil Procedure Section 664.6." See Sayta v. Chu (2017) 17 Cal. App. 5th 960 ("Wackeen does not stand for the proposition that parties may confer jurisdiction on trial courts by including language in a settlement agreement but not asking the court to retain jurisdiction. Settlement language purporting to vest the trial court with retained jurisdiction after the dismissal is a nullity: Subject matter jurisdiction cannot be conferred by consent, waiver or estoppel"); see also Mesa RHF Partners, L.P. v. City of Los Angeles (2019) 33 Cal. App. 5th 913 ("The request to the court that it retain jurisdiction under section 664.6 must be made by the parties --- In this case, the parties could have easily invoked section 664.6 by filing a stipulation and proposed order either attaching a copy of the settlement agreement AND requesting that the trial court retain jurisdiction under section 664.6 or a stipulation and proposed order signed by the parties noting the settlement AND requesting that the trial court retain jurisdiction under section 664.6.") PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUN WAH MOON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/18/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) WHO is the trustee of Frank Toy Moon's trust? If Frank Toy Moon's will is not being probated, identifying the executor named in the will is not sufficient. (2) Neither supplement filed on 1/29/24 supplements the petition with the names, ages, relationships to this decedent, and addresses of the trustee of Frank Toy Moon's trust or of Linda Sue Moon's heirs. (The supplement filed on 10/23/23 identified two children of Linda Sue Moon and one son of Frank Toy Moon but did not set for their ages or addresses.) (3) There is no Exhibit A attached to the supplement filed on 1/29/24 that refers to an Exhibit A. (4) It is as yet unclear if all interested parties have waived bond. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues by 3/8/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT BENJAMIN CROSBY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/11/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 11/27/2023 - see also tentative ruling for 01/17/2024 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-147 form is on file. 3) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be filed confidentially and conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed or attached to any e-filed document). 4) No DE-121 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 5) No proof of publication is on file. 6) Item 3e(3) (rather than 3e(2)) of the petition must be checked, if the decedent died intestate. 7) Item 3g(2)(b) must be checked. 8) Item 5a(3) or (4) must be checked. 9) Item 8: i) The petitioner must be listed. ii) The names and dates of death of all deceased children of the decedent must be listed. iii) The decedent's child who was a parent of each grandchild listed at item 8 must be identified.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAXINE GRODJINSKY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/11/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Until e-filing becomes available for this, Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (3) Item 5a(3) or (4) of the petition must be completed. (4) Item 8 of the petition must list all persons mentioned in the will, including all beneficiaries and executors, not just the sole heir. (5) If any of the persons mentioned in the will or otherwise entitled to notice is deceased, the date of that person's death must be set forth. [LRSF 14.18 C.] (6) If any of the other persons mentioned in the will is still living, that person must receive notice. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/1/24. (The petitioner must sign and verify a supplement to his petition to address the missing allegations in his petition.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ZELLA MAE BAILEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/13/2024. The following issues must be addressed: 1) Is Lucretia Bailey listed at item 8 the decedent's only child identified in the will as the decedent's daughter who passed away in 2020? 2) Are the petitioner and 2 other grandchildren of the decedent listed at item 8 Lucretia Bailey's children? 3) Is Billy Bailey listed at item 8 the decedent's spouse identified in the will as the decedent's husband who passed away on 10/06/2021? 4) An AMENDED proof of holographic instrument must be filed to attach a copy of the will which must be marked "Attachment 4". 5) Notice of the continued hearing on the seoncd amended petition must be mailed and published. The words "SECOND AMENDED" (petition) must be added where applicable on the DE-121 form to be mailed and published.
